---
title: "Political Analysis"
date: now
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
    code-tools: true
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: sentence
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE, 
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.height = 2.75, 
  fig.width = 4.25,
  fig.env = 'figure',
  fig.pos = 'h',
  fig.align = 'center')

library(tidyverse)
cb_palette <- c("black", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7")
scale_colour_discrete <- function(...) scale_colour_manual(values = cb_palette, ...)
scale_fill_discrete   <- function(...) scale_fill_manual(values = cb_palette, ...)
theme_set(theme_bw())
```



Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
#Dataset
library(tidyverse)
pulse <- read.csv("https://mac-stat.github.io/data/pulse_of_the_nation_processed.csv")
```


```{r}
head(pulse)
```

```{r}
# Dataset for our model 
pulse2 <- pulse %>%
  mutate(
    party_clean = str_trim(str_to_lower(as.character(party))),
    party_binary = case_when(
    party_clean == "democrat" ~ "Democrats",
    party_clean %in% c("republican", "independent") ~ "Non-democrats", TRUE ~ "Non-democrats"),
    party_binary = factor(party_binary, levels = c("Democrats", "Non-democrats"))) %>%
    select(-party_clean)  
```

```{r}
head(pulse2)
```


```{r}
#Adding the "TRUE" and "FALSE" components 
pulse_final_version <- pulse2 %>%
  mutate(Democrats = (party == "Democrat"))
```

```{r}
head(pulse_final_version)
```


```{r}
# Used in our table 1 
pulse_final_version %>% 
  count(Democrats)
```

```{r}
# Used in our table 1
table_info <- pulse_final_version %>%
  group_by(education) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    percentage_dem = mean(party_binary == "Democrats") * 100,
    percentage_non_dem = mean(party_binary == "Non-democrats") * 100)
```

```{r}
table_info
```
```{r}
pulse_final_version %>% 
  count(education, party_binary)
```


```{r}
# Used in our table 1 
summary_income_by_party <- pulse_final_version %>%
group_by(party_binary) %>%
summarise(
    mean_income = round(mean(income, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    sd_income = round(sd(income, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    total = n())
```


```{r}
summary_income_by_party
```


```{r}
# Results section 
# Converting party to 0/1 numeric, supposing "Democrat" = 1 and "Not Democrat" = 0
pulse_with_numbers <- pulse_final_version %>%
mutate(party_num = if_else(party == "Democrat", 1, 0))

#Used results 
# Logistic regression
partydata <- glm(party_num ~ education + income, data = pulse_with_numbers, family = binomial)
summary(partydata)
```

```{r}
exp(coef(partydata))
```


```{r}
# Figure 1
pulse_final_version %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = Democrats)) +
  geom_bar()+
  labs(title = "Number of Non-Democrats vs Democrats")
```

```{r}
# Figure 2 
pulse_final_version %>% 
  mutate(education = factor(education,levels = c("High school", "Some college", "College degree", "Graduate degree", "Other"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x= education, fill = Democrats))+ 
  geom_bar(position = "fill") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Education levels by party affiliation")
```

```{r}
# Figure 3
pulse_final_version %>%
  ggplot(aes(y = as.numeric(Democrats), x = income)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", se = FALSE, method.args = list(family = "binomial"),
  fullrange = TRUE) +
  labs(y = "Probability value") +
  lims(x = c(1, 400))+
  labs(title = "Probability of being a Democrat by Income per $100")
```

```{r}
# Figure 4
pulse_final_version %>% 
  mutate(education = factor(education,levels = c("High school", "Some college", "College degree", "Graduate degree", "Other"))) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(y = as.numeric(Democrats), x = income, color = education)) + 
  geom_smooth(method = "glm", se = FALSE, method.args = list(family = "binomial"),
  fullrange = TRUE) +
  labs(y = "Probability value") + 
  lims(x = c(0, 400))+
  labs(title = "Probability of being a Democrat based on income and education level")
```

```{r}
# Figure 5 
pulse_final_version %>% 
  mutate(education = factor(education,levels = c("High school", "Some college", "College degree", "Graduate degree", "Other"))) %>% 
  filter(gender != "Other", age < 80) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = education, y = age, fill = gender)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 40, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(title = "Education levels by gender and age")
```

```{r}
#F Test (determining the impact of education on the overal model)  
partymodel1<-glm(Democrats~education+income, data=pulse_final_version,family= "binomial")
partymodel3<-glm(Democrats~education, data=pulse_final_version,family= "binomial")
ls(pulse_final_version)
summary(partymodel3)
anova(partymodel3,partymodel1)
```

```{r}
partymodel1<-glm(Democrats~education+income, data=pulse_final_version,family= "binomial")
partymodel3<-glm(Democrats~income, data=pulse_final_version,family= "binomial")
ls(pulse_final_version)
summary(partymodel3)
anova(partymodel3, partymodel1)
```

#Model evaluation 
```{r}
#Overall accuracy 
(146+278)/1000 
```


```{r}
#Sensitivity 
278/329
```


```{r}
#Specificity 
146/671
```
